instant.evidence: Static curated catalog; no runtime latency surface.

natural.evidence: Data catalog of capabilities, not a conversational agent.

permitted.evidence: Curation is governed by config/library_approvers.json; each entry carries provenance (source, source_url).

adaptive.evidence: Auto re-scanned: registry tracks last_scan_status and sources_scanned; entries carry last_verified and are refreshed on scan.

contextual.evidence: This is the catalog itself; retrieval/grounding happens in the consumers that read it, not here.

transparent.evidence: Every entry carries source, source_url, and last_verified; the registry carries last_updated and last_scan_status.

governance.evidence: Curated from approved sources under the approver matrix.

observability.evidence: Scan status + per-entry verification timestamps make staleness visible.

availability.evidence: A committed catalog file, not a hosted service.

lexicon.evidence: Canonical skill schema: consistent id/name/category/tags vocabulary across entries.

solid.evidence: Entries conform to skill.schema.json; scan status is tracked per refresh.

layers.name: Agent-Aware Governance
layers.how: Curation governed by the approver matrix with per-entry provenance.

layers.name: Observability & Feedback
layers.how: Scan status + last_verified timestamps expose freshness.

layers.name: Multi-Agent Orchestration
layers.how: Capability catalog that downstream agents draw on.
